assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md classifies comment 30001 as 'code change request' (line 7: 'Classification: code change request') and states 'Action: Sub-task created to wrap the three UPDATE operations in a database transaction' (line 15). The file subtask-30001.md exists confirming sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies comment 30002 as 'suggestion' (line 7). The reasoning section explicitly references suggestive language: 'The reviewer uses suggestive language: "The migration should also add an index on deleted_at for the sbom table."' It further explains: 'This is a suggestion because: 1. The missing index does not cause incorrect behavior -- it affects performance only. 2. The reviewer provides an example but does not demand the change as a blocker.'

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md classifies comment 30003 as 'nit' (line 7: 'Classification: nit') and states 'Action: No sub-task created. Minor style feedback that does not affect correctness.' (line 18). No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md classifies comment 30004 as 'question' (line 7: 'Classification: question') and states 'Action: No sub-task created. This is a clarification question.' (line 19). No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md (the only sub-task) contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 11), '## Implementation Notes' (line 14), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 22). All sections have substantive content.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section (line 36) with the exact URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 37).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 29) with 'Original review comment (ID 30001):' followed by the quoted original review comment text about wrapping three UPDATE statements in a database transaction (lines 30-31).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only: report.md, review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, and subtask-30001.md. All are markdown analysis/report files. No code files (.rs, .toml, .sql, etc.) exist in the outputs. The report and classification files are all read-only analysis artifacts.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md summarizes all four: '1 code change request (comment 30001: transaction wrapping); 1 suggestion (comment 30002: add index); 1 nit (comment 30003: context message); 1 question (comment 30004: GET behavior).' This confirms all comments were processed.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merge, or merge commands. The overall verdict is 'FAIL' (line 17: '### Overall: FAIL'), and the report identifies issues requiring attention. There is no indication that any merge action was taken or attempted.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test changes are in new file `tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`; 5 new test functions added, no tests modified or removed. |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a dedicated 'Convention upgrade check' paragraph (lines 19-20): 'The Style/Conventions sub-agent checked for upgrade eligibility. No CONVENTIONS.md is available for the target repository. The PR diff contains only the single new migration file, providing no codebase pattern evidence. The suggestion was NOT upgraded -- it remains classified as a suggestion.' This explicitly evaluates convention upgrade eligibility.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states 'Action: No sub-task created. The suggestion is valid but optional.' (line 21). No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. The reasoning explains: 'No CONVENTIONS.md is available to back up this suggestion as a required practice' and 'The PR diff contains only one migration file, so there is no demonstrable codebase pattern.'

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS (5 tests with distinct algorithms). Test Documentation: PASS (all 5 tests have doc comments). Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews). |' The Eval Quality is explicitly marked as N/A with the parenthetical explanation '(no eval result reviews)'.
